text: Great. One last thing before we drive — what's your asking price flexibility? I see you've got her listed at $19,995. Is that firm, or is there any room to negotiate if everything checks out on the test drive?

speaker: seller

args.message: Good question — I appreciate directness. Look, $19,995 is a fair price for a Wrangler in this condition with these miles. I've got some flexibility, sure, but not a ton. These 3.6L Wranglers move fast around here — I saw one very similar go at auction last week for almost $20,500. That said, let's take her out, let the vehicle sell itself, and then we can talk numbers if you're genuinely interested. If she checks out the way I know she will, we can probably find a deal that works for both of us. Fair enough?

speaker: seller

args.message: Great — glad you felt how well she runs! I've had her on the lot for about three weeks now. She came to me from a private owner who was relocating out of state for work, needed to move quick and didn't have time to deal with selling privately. So, where are you at mentally? You interested in making this work?
